AI infrastructure is now measured in gigawatts, not server racks.

The numbers this week should change how every executive thinks about compute strategy.

TWEET 2
NVIDIA invested $2B in CoreWeave in Jan 2026. Target: 5 gigawatts of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave's contracted backlog hit $66.8B by end of 2025.

This is infrastructure at industrial scale.

---

TWEET 3
Meta and AMD signed a 5-year deal: up to 6 gigawatts of Instinct GPUs. That is more power capacity than some small countries consume.

AI compute is now a strategic asset class.

TWEET 4
Neoclouds like CoreWeave earned Gold-tier ratings alongside Azure and Oracle. The AI compute market is fragmenting fast.

Single-hyperscaler strategies are becoming a concentration risk.

## Fact-check notes
- NVIDIA invested $2 billion in CoreWeave in January 2026 to accelerate buildout toward 5 gigawatts of AI factories by 2030 (Source: The New Stack, AI Cloud Taxonomy 2026)
- CoreWeave reported a contracted revenue backlog of $66.8 billion as of December 31, 2025, up from $55.6 billion at end of Q3 2025 (Source: The New Stack, AI Cloud Taxonomy 2026)
- Meta and AMD announced a 5-year partnership on Feb 24, 2026, with AMD supplying up to 6 gigawatts of Instinct GPUs (Source: devFlokers, AI Breakthroughs March 2026)
- CoreWeave reached more than 850 megawatts of connected capacity (Source: The New Stack, AI Cloud Taxonomy 2026)
